scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, researched directly against the live Younium developer portal (developer.younium.com/api-s/api-2.1/*, developer.younium.com/api-s/api-2.0/currency for the resources not yet migrated to 2.1's docs tree). Younium documents 29 top-level resource categories (Accounts, Bookings, ChartOfAccounts, Countries, Currency, CustomFieldConfigurations, DocumentTemplate, ExchangeRate, InvoiceBatches, InvoiceBatchGroups, Invoices, Journals, Measurements, Metrics, Orders, Payments, PaymentTerms, Products, Quotes, Reports, RevenueSchedule, SalesOrders, SimpleProducts, Subscriptions, TaxTemplates, UnitOfMeasurements, Usage, Users, Webhooks). This pass expands the 3 legacy-parity read streams (accounts, subscriptions, invoices) to 7 read streams (adding products, payment_terms, currencies, webhooks — the resources with a plain top-level GET list endpoint and record shape a warehouse destination can use directly) and adds 5 write actions covering the account/subscription/invoice lifecycle mutations expressible with a single JSON/no body PATCH or POST (create_account, update_account, cancel_subscription, post_invoice, cancel_invoice). The remaining ~23 resource categories and every list/detail/CRUD/sub-resource endpoint under them are excluded below with a specific reason each; none are silently dropped.

excluded.reason: single-account detail; identical record shape to the accounts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
irreversibly deletes a billing account and its history; not modeled as a write action this pass (breadth-vs-cost triage on the riskiest mutation in the resource)
stores a customer's raw online-payment-method details (card/bank token); a PCI-adjacent credential-shaped payload, not a plain data mutation
updates stored online-payment-method details; same PCI-adjacent scope concern as the create endpoint
deletes stored online-payment-method details; same PCI-adjacent scope concern as the create endpoint
account-scoped subscription list; same record shape as the subscriptions stream (which reads every subscription, account_id included via computed_fields), not a distinct resource
account-scoped invoice list; same record shape as the invoices stream, not a distinct resource
SalesOrders is a separate undocumented-here resource category; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
a computed account-summary aggregate view, not a syncable object list
initiates a GoCardless payment-mandate collection flow for the account; a payment-provider-scoped action, not a plain data mutation
single-subscription detail; identical record shape to the subscriptions list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

full subscription-creation payload (order lines, charge plans, discounts) is a compound multi-section body the dialect's flat record-to-JSON-body write model cannot faithfully validate; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
generic subscription field patch overlapping the more specific, already-covered cancel/renew/activate lifecycle actions; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
irreversibly deletes a subscription record outright (distinct from the reversible-intent cancel action already covered); not modeled this pass
subscription-renewal lifecycle action; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage (cancel_subscription covers the highest-risk lifecycle mutation this pass targets)
subscription-activation lifecycle action; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
reverts a subscription's most recent lifecycle change; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
mid-term subscription change (order-line-shaped compound body); same compound-body concern as subscription creation
compound renew+change lifecycle action with an order-line-shaped body; same compound-body concern as subscription creation
subscription version-history list; a narrower audit-trail sub-resource, not a primary syncable object
current-version detail; same record shape as a subscriptions list item, already covered by that stream
single historical version detail; a narrower cut of the versions list already excluded above
subscription milestone sub-resource list; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
sets a subscription milestone; a narrow sub-resource mutation, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
adds an order-discount line to a subscription; a narrow order-line mutation, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-invoice detail; identical record shape to the invoices list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
related-invoice list; same record shape as the invoices stream, not a distinct resource
invoice-line sub-resource filtered by charge type; a narrower cut below the invoice/document level, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
usage-linked invoice lookup keyed by an id from an unmigrated Usage resource; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
invoice-line sub-resource filtered by usage id; same triage as the ChargeType cut above
downloads the invoice PDF/attachment binary, not a JSON record
generates a signed, time-limited download URL for the invoice attachment, not a syncable data record
invoice attachment metadata list; a narrow document-management sub-resource, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
cross-invoice posted-document attachment listing; same document-management triage as the per-invoice attachments list
downloads an attachment binary by id, not a JSON record
online-payment attempt log sub-resource; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
generic invoice field patch overlapping the more specific, already-covered post/cancel lifecycle actions; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
manually marks an invoice paid outside Younium's own payment-collection flow, a financial-reconciliation action gated behind billing-admin scope
creates an on-account (prepayment) invoice, a distinct compound invoice-creation shape; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
issues a credit memo against a posted invoice, a distinct financial-document-creation shape from plain cancel; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
generates invoices from a set of orders, a compound multi-order operation; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
triggers an actual Stripe payment charge against the invoice; a real-money payment-processing action gated behind billing-admin scope
